rows.can_do: Can use 'and' to join a limited range of common adjectives.
rows.example:
The teachers are very nice and friendly . (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2007; Polish; Pass)

I like my house because it is big and comfortable . (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2007; Polish; Pass)

You can buy Turkish and English products as well. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; Polish)

rows.can_do: Can use 'very' with a limited range of common gradable adjectives.
rows.example:
I like Croydon because it's very quiet and very nice. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; Farsi)

My neighbours are very friendly. (A1; Slovak)

rows.can_do: Can use a limited range of adjectives attributively, before a noun. ► noun phrases
rows.example:
We have a big garden and a small swimming pool. (Arabic - Other, A1 BREAKTHROUGH, Pass, 2009)

My name is Bogdan. I am your new neighbour. (Polish, A1 BREAKTHROUGH, Pass, 2008)

I like my home because I have a nice park next to my house. (Farsi, A1 BREAKTHROUGH, Pass, 2009)

rows.can_do: Can use a limited range of adjectives predicatively, after 'be'.
rows.example:
I like my home because my bedroom is big. (Turkish, A1 BREAKTHROUGH, Pass, 2009)

This place is lovely. (Polish, A1 BREAKTHROUGH, Pass, 2007)

My kitchen is nice. (Farsi, A1 BREAKTHROUGH, Pass, 2009)

This place is beautiful. (Tagalog, A1 BREAKTHROUGH, Pass, 2007)

rows.can_do: Can use the irregular superlative adjective 'best' in the phrase 'my best friend'.
rows.example:
She's my best friend. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2006; Polish; Pass)

Shahin is my best friend. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2006; Urdu; Pass)

rows.can_do: Can modify adverbs of time and degree with 'very'.
rows.example:
I hope you will be fine very soon. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2009; Urdu; Pass)

I like my job very much. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2007; Mandarin; Pass)

clusters.cluster_key: ADVERBS | adverbs and adverb phrases: types and meanings | USE
clusters.sub_category: adverbs and adverb phrases: types and meanings

rows.can_do: Can use 'soon' in the phrases 'See you soon' and 'Get well soon', as a signing-off device.
rows.example:
Take care. See you soon. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2009; Turkish; Pass)

Get well soon. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2009; Polish; Pass)

rows.can_do: Can use a limited range of adverbs ('here', 'there') to indicate place.
rows.example:
My girlfriend is here with me. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2007; Slovak; Pass)

I was born there and I lived there over twenty years. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2007; Spanish - Other; Pass)

I go there every year with my friends. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2011; Bulgarian; Pass)

rows.can_do: Can use 'really' and 'very much' with verbs expressing preference ('like', 'love'), for emphasis.
rows.example:
I really like theatre and a classical music. (Poland; A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2010; Polish; Pass)

I really love my space! (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2009; Portuguese; Pass)

I like my garden very much. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2009; Bengali; Pass)

rows.can_do: Can use 'very' to make adjectives stronger.
rows.example:
He's a very good man. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2006; Mandarin; Pass)

I'm very happy in my home. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2009; Spanish - Latin American; Pass)

rows.can_do: Can use a limited range of adverbs ('always', 'sometimes') to talk about frequency.
rows.example:
I like Dorchester's people, they are always very friendly and polite. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2007; Russian; Pass)

I always see Safora because she lives near my home. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2006; Farsi; Pass)

Sometimes I go to the beach. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2011; Arabic - Other; Pass)

rows.can_do: Can use 'very' to modify common gradable adjectives.
rows.example:
My home is very nice. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2009; Italian; Pass)

My teacher is very good. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2007; Gujarati; Pass)

rows.can_do: Can use 'very' to modify time adverbs. ► adverb phrases
rows.example:
I hope you get well very soon. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2009; Bengali; Pass)

I don't like it at college when I have a lesson very early. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2007; Polish; Pass)

rows.can_do: Can use place adverbs ('here', 'there') as complements of verbs.
rows.example:
I am happy here because people are very nice. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2007; Hindi; Pass)

I hope you will be there. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2011; Portuguese; Pass)

You must go there. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2008; Polish; Pass)

rows.guideword: FORM: DEGREE and TIME, MODIFYING VERBS
rows.can_do: Can use degree and time adverbs to modify verbs.
rows.example:
I really like Japanese music. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2008; Chinese; Pass)

I like my college very much. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2007; Polish; Fail)

I always go to the park with my children. We sometimes go to the shopping centre. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2010; Arabic - Other; Pass)

rows.can_do: Can use a limited range of adverbs in front position.
rows.example:
Sometimes I go to the beach. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2011; Arabic - Other; Pass)

I like writing and speaking but sometimes I like listening. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2007; Somali; Pass)

You can bring some music . Maybe we can dance. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2008; Polish; Pass)

rows.can_do: Can use a limited range of degree adverbs before an adjective.
rows.example:
My home is very light and very comfortable. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2009; Polish; Pass)

I have a really good friend, her name is Dylma. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2006; Polish; Pass)

rows.can_do: Can use frequency adverbs in the usual mid position, between the subject and the main verb.
rows.example:
In my free time I always listen to music, watch TV, clean my room ... (Spain; A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2010; Spanish - European; Pass)

She sometimes goes to the cinema and to the disco. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2006; Polish; Pass)

rows.can_do: Can use 'really' in the usual mid-position, between the subject and the main verb.
rows.example:
I really love walking. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2011; Polish; Pass)

I really like my kitchen, because it is big. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2009; Somali; Pass)

rows.can_do: Can use time adverbs in the usual end position.
rows.example:
Get well soon. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2009; Nepali; Pass)

I hope to see you soon. (Pakistan; A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2010; Urdu; Pass)

… because I not working also I will wake up late . (Pakistan; A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2010; Urdu; Pass)

rows.can_do: Can use place adverbs in the usual end position.
rows.example:
I have many friends there. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2007; Polish; Fail)

I like this place because my parents live there. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2007; Czech; Fail)

rows.can_do: Can omit the subject in a second main declarative clause when the subject is obvious or the same as the first clause.
rows.example:
She likes to listen to music and watch TV. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2006; Urdu; Pass)

After break we go to the class and play some games or study. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2007; Polish; Pass)

Sometimes I go to the park with my friend and play tennis and football. (Poland; A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2010; Polish; Pass)

I can clean or cook for you. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2009; Romanian; Pass)

rows.can_do: Can combine two main declarative clauses using co-ordinating conjunctions ('and', 'but', 'or').
rows.example:
I like my college and I like my class. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2007; Gujarati; Pass)

On Sunday I visit my friends, but I often stay at home and I read books. (Poland; A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2010; Polish; Pass)

I like English lessons but I don't like physics. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2007; Polish; Pass)

I watch television, I read magazines or I sleep. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2007; Slovak; Pass)

rows.can_do: Can form simple affirmative declarative clauses.
rows.example:
I study English at college. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2007; Mandarin; Pass)

My friend is a nurse. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2006; Panjabi; Pass)

I'm living in Manchester. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2009; Kurdish; Pass)

It's very cheap and you can buy all you need. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2008; Italian; Fail)

He is very nice but sometimes he is a little crazy. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2006; Italian; Pass)

rows.can_do: Can form a simple negative declarative clause with lexical 'be' + 'not' or 'n't'.
rows.example:
My college is not very big. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2007; Polish; Pass)

This place isn't big. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2007; Lithuanian; Pass)

I'm not bad. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2006; Panjabi; Pass)

rows.can_do: Can form simple negative declarative clauses.
rows.example:
I don't like maths. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2007; Farsi; Pass)

I don't have children. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2008; Polish; Pass)

I don't know. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2009; French; Pass)

rows.guideword: FORM: AFFIRMATIVE DECLARATIVE, MODAL AUXILIARY VERBS
rows.can_do: Can form an affirmative declarative clause with modal verbs.
rows.example:
I can go to see her tomorrow. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2009; Panjabi; Pass)

I will visit you soon. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2009; Kurdish; Pass)

rows.can_do: Can form interrogative clauses with 'be'.
rows.example:
How are you? (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2009; Farsi; Pass)

Are you ok? (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2011; Thai; Pass)

rows.can_do: Can use a finite subordinate clause with 'because', after a main clause, to introduce reasons.
rows.example:
I love her because she is friendly. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2006; Bengali; Pass)

I like my television because it is big and modern. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2009; Arabic - Other; Pass)

rows.can_do: Can use single word conjunctions ('and, but, or') to combine phrases.
rows.example:
My home is very light and very comfortable. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2009; Polish; Pass)

I live with my mother and her boyfriend. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2009; Polish; Pass)

My house is small but very nice. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2009; Pashto; Pass)

She likes to go to the cinema or to the theatre. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2006; French; Pass)

rows.can_do: Can use single word conjunctions ('and, but, or') to combine sentences.
rows.example:
I like it because there are lots of shops, it is not too busy and there is lots of parking. And I like my home because it's not too big and it's a very nice flat. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2009; Tamil; Pass)

I am from Estonia. Estonia it is a small country near the Baltic Sea. But now I live in Dorchester, because I work here. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2007; Russian; Pass)

I go shopping on Ladypool Road. Or sometimes I go to my local shop.  (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2008; Panjabi; Pass)

rows.can_do: Can use single word conjunctions ('and, but, or') to combine clauses.
rows.example:
He is a journalist and he likes everything about cinema. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2006; Italian; Pass)

There are lots of shops in the town and the town is only five minutes away. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2008; Urdu; Pass)

I have visited a lot of places, but the best place is Thailand. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2007; Slovak; Pass)

I like to eat pasta or pizza, but I'm easy about food, I can eat anything. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2006; French; Pass)

We love to go to the cinema, or just sit together and have a laugh. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2006; Portuguese; Pass)

rows.can_do: Can use single word conjunctions ('and, but, or') to connect single nouns and adjectives.
rows.example:
I like chicken and rice. I don't like meat and fish. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2006; Kurdish; Pass)

My street is quiet and friendly. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2008; Polish; Pass)

On Monday and Wednesday I have English and maths. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2007; Polish; Pass)

I like my kitchen, it is small but nice. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2009; Polish; Pass)

I like cheese pizza or chicken. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2006; French; Pass)

I like to eat soup, eggs, meat and salad or banana and cheese. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2006; Polish; Pass)

rows.can_do: Can use 'and' and 'or' before the final item in a list.
rows.example:
I like to eat chicken, mushrooms and some vegetables. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2006; Polish; Pass)

I have three rooms; a kitchen, living room, bathroom and toilet. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2009; Polish; Pass)

I go to college every Tuesday, Thursday and Friday morning, (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2007; French; Pass)

I really like my English course because there are very good students from other countries, for example from Italy, Portugal, China and India. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2007; Slovak; Pass)
I like to eat steak, vegetables, potatoes or rice. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2006; Polish; Pass)

rows.can_do: Can use 'but' to add an unexpected contrast.
rows.example:
I really like my home, it is very small but it is very easy to clean. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2009; Urdu; Pass)

I like my home, it's small but so comfortable, and the decoration is beautiful. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2009; Portuguese; Pass)

rows.can_do: Can use 'because' as a subordinating conjunction to introduce a subordinate clause.
rows.example:
I want a big garden because I have children. (Arabic - Other)

She likes meeting other people because she wants to improve her English. (Nepali)

rows.can_do: Can use articles 'the', 'a' and 'an' before nouns. ► noun phrases
rows.example:
She lives in a small flat in the town - it's great! (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2006; German; Pass)

I have an appointment with my G.P. at 5.45 pm. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2006; Turkish; Pass)

rows.can_do: Can use 'a' + 'very' + adjectives in basic noun phrases. ► adverbs ►  noun phrases
rows.example:
He's a very good man. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2006; Mandarin; Pass)

It is a very beautiful place. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2007; Russian; Pass)

It's a very nice town. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2007; Slovak; Pass)

rows.can_do: Can use 'a' and 'an' before adjectives in a noun phrase. ► adjectives ►  noun phrases
rows.example:
You can make a quick snack, for example a sandwich. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2006; German; Pass)

We have a big garden and a small swimming pool. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2009; Arabic - Other; Pass)

I like it because it is an interesting place. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2007; Romanian; Fail)

It's an old house, about 50 years old. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2007; Arabic - Other; Fail)

rows.can_do: Can use 'the' in prepositional phrases relating to time.
rows.example:
I work at the weekend and I'm very busy from Monday to Friday. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2008; Polish; Pass)

I can come to see you on Friday in the morning. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2009; Thai; Pass)

rows.can_do: Can use no article before a limited range of nouns in some fixed expressions with prepositions.
rows.example:
If you are at home, I can come tomorrow. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2009; Farsi; Pass)

We are going to the sports centre by car. (Brazil; A2 WAYSTAGE; 2008; Portuguese; Pass)

I go to college every day. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2007; Polish; Pass)

rows.can_do: Can use no article before a limited range of singular, plural and uncountable nouns when referring to things in general.
rows.example:
In my free time I usually play football. (Poland; A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2010; Polish; Pass)

In my free time usually I stay at home. I watch TV, read books… (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2010; Arabic - Other; Pass)

I like rice and sweet dishes. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2006; Urdu; Pass)

rows.can_do: Can use 'this' with singular nouns. ► noun phrases ►  pronouns: demonstrative
rows.example:
I like this place because is very nice and quiet. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2007; Polish; Pass)

If you like, then we can go this Saturday. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2011; Lithuanian; Pass)

rows.can_do: Can use 'this' in a limited range of contexts to refer to places from the speaker's or writer's point of view. ► noun phrases ►  pronouns: demonstrative
rows.example:
I want to let you know about this area and the shops near here. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2008; ; Pass)

The football class and tennis class are the best things about this college. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2007; Spanish - Other; Pass)

I like this place because there are many cultures. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2007; Dutch; Pass)

rows.can_do: Can use 'this' with time and date words to refer to 'the one that's coming'.
rows.example:
… this summer I want to spend my holiday in my country, Bulgaria. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2011; Bengali; Pass)

I have a good plan for this weekend. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2011; Polish; Pass)

I'll call you this evening. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2009; Turkish; Pass)

rows.can_do: Can use possessive determiners 'my', 'your', 'his', 'her' and 'our' before nouns. ► noun phrases ► possessive pronouns
rows.example:
I live with my family. My home is a flat. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2009; Urdu; Pass)

… my neighbours are very nice and helpful. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2009; Panjabi; Pass)

I like my teachers. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2007; Farsi; Pass)

I live with my mother and her boyfriend. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2009; Polish; Pass)

Our house has four rooms, one bathroom and one toilet. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2009; Slovak; Pass)

rows.can_do: Can use a limited range of quantifying determiners with plural nouns ('some', 'lots of', 'a lot of' and numbers).
rows.example:
Near my home there are some shops. (Portuguese, A1 BREAKTHROUGH, Pass, 2009)

My house has got three rooms. (Polish, A1 BREAKTHROUGH, Pass, 2009) 

I had a lot of friends in this town. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2007; Pashto; Pass)

For homework we have to write lots of things. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2007; ; Pass)

rows.can_do: Can use a limited range of quantifying determiners with singular nouns ('a', 'every').► determiners: articles
rows.example:
In my home are 3 bedrooms. I have a big kitchen and small toilet. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2009; Polish; Pass)

The first floor has two bedrooms and a big bathroom with a bath. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2009; Romanian; Pass)

I go to India every year to see my friends and family. (Panjabi, A1 BREAKTHROUGH, Pass, 2007)

I go to college every day. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2007; Polish; Pass)

clusters.cluster_key: FUTURE | future simple (with will and shall) | FORM

rows.can_do: Can use the affirmative forms 'will' and ''ll'.
rows.example:
I will see you soon. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2009; Urdu; Pass)
 I'll come at 7 o'clock. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2006; Others; Pass)

clusters.cluster_key: FUTURE | future simple (with will and shall) | USE

rows.can_do: Can use 'will' and ''ll' to talk about plans and intentions.
rows.example:
I will try to come to see you tomorrow. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2009; Romanian; Pass)

I'll bring a football ball with me. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2011; Bulgarian; Pass)

rows.can_do: Can use the affirmative form.
rows.example:
You can see a lot of special places in Wroclaw. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH, 2007, Polish)

You can make a quick snack, for example, a sandwich. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH, 2006, German)

rows.can_do: Can use the affirmative question form.
rows.example:
Can you come for dinner Monday at 6.00 pm? (Arabic - Other, A1 BREAKTHROUGH, Pass, 2008)

Can we go to the park on the Sunday? (Somali, A1 BREAKTHROUGH, Pass, 2011)

rows.can_do: Can use the negative forms.
rows.example:
I cannot come to see you. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH, 2009, Bengali)

I'm very nervous and I can't say anything. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH Polish)

rows.can_do: Can use 'can' to make an offer.
rows.example:
Can I help you? (A1 BREAKTHROUGH, 2009, Lithuanian)

What can I do for you? (A1 BREAKTHROUGH, 2009, Turkish)

How can I help you? (A1 BREAKTHROUGH, 2009, Polish)

If you want, I can come to your house and I can help you with for example some housework on Sunday this weekend. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2009; Czech; Pass)

rows.can_do: Can use 'can' to talk about ability to do something.
rows.example:
I can cook. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH, 2009, Turkish)

I can write English. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH, 2007, Thai)

[talking about someone with a physical disability] He cannot go up stairs. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH, 2009, Urdu)

[talking about reading in English] I can’t read very well. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH, 2007, Polish)

rows.can_do: Can use 'can' to make a request.
rows.example:
Can you come on Sunday at seven o’clock? (A1 BREAKTHROUGH, 2010, Arabic)

Can you call me? (A1 BREAKTHROUGH, 2009, Lithuanian)

rows.can_do: Can use 'can' to talk about possibility.
rows.example:
The shop is next to the post office. There you can find milk, juices and sweets but you can't buy meat. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH, 2008, Spanish - Other)

I cannot see you today. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH, 2009, Farsi)

I'm sorry I can't see you now because I am very busy at work. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH, 2009, Mandarin)

rows.can_do: Can use the affirmative forms.
rows.example:
I will be at your house at 7.30 because I have an appointment before. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2006; Russian; Pass)

I'll come to see you tomorrow morning. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2009; Nepali; Pass)

rows.can_do: Can use 'will' to talk about plans and intentions.
rows.example:
I will bring some music to the dinner. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2008; Bengali; Pass)

I'll see you tomorrow. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2006; Polish; Pass)

rows.can_do: Can use the affirmative form with 'like'.
rows.example:
I'd like to invite you to dinner. (Farsi, A1 BREAKTHROUGH, Pass, 2008)

I would like to eat some Spanish food. (Italian, A1 BREAKTHROUGH, Pass, 2006)

rows.can_do: Can use 'would like to invite' to make invitations.
rows.example:
I would like to invite you to dinner in my house on Saturday at 7 30 pm. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2010; Others; Pass)

Dear Neighbours, I would like to invite you to my home for dinner. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2008; Arabic - Other; Pass)

rows.can_do: Can use 'would like' to talk about wishes and preferences.
rows.example:
If possible, I'd like to come to see you on Tuesday afternoon. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2009; French; Pass)

I would like to eat chicken, potatoes and some vegetables. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2006; Nepali; Pass)

rows.can_do: Can form negative statements of main verbs in the present simple with 'don't' + main verb. ► present simple ► can
rows.example:
I don't have a dining room, so we eat in the living room. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2009; Romanian; Pass)

We don't like other lessons, for example: French language. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2007; Polish; Pass)

rows.can_do: Can form negative statements of main verb 'be', with contracted and uncontracted forms.
rows.example:
My college is not very big. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2007; Polish; Pass)

I know you are not well. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2009; French; Pass)

I'm not a good swimmer. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2011; Romanian; Pass)

This place isn't big. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2007; Lithuanian; Pass)

rows.can_do: Can use negative form 'can’t'.
rows.example:
In winter, we can't go visiting because it is too cold. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2011; Nepali; Pass)

I can't find my CD. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2008; Romanian; Pass)

rows.can_do: Can form simple noun phrases by pre-modifying plural nouns with an adjective and no determiner.
rows.example:
I have new neighbours. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2008; Gujarati; Pass)

They're nice people. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2009; Arabic - Other; Pass)

This place has nice shops. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2007; Polish; Pass)

I like reading interesting books in the Library. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2007; Polish; Pass)

rows.can_do: Can form simple noun phrases by pre-modifying singular and plural nouns with an adjective after a determiner.
rows.example:
Martin is my best friend. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2006; Slovak; Pass)

We have a big garden and a small swimming pool. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2009; Arabic - Other; Pass)

Her favourite colours are blue and black. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2006; Hungarian; Pass)

rows.can_do: Can form simple noun phrases with a limited range of determiners + singular and plural nouns. ► Nouns and noun phrases: functions
rows.example:
There is also a park near my house. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2009; Pashto; Pass)

My husband is a teacher. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2009; Korean; Pass)

That's why I like this place. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2007; Urdu; Pass)

I go to college every day. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2007; Polish; Pass)

rows.can_do: Can form noun phrases by pre-modifying a limited range of nouns with another noun.
rows.example:
Every Friday I go swimming and play computer games. (Poland; A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2010; Polish; Pass)

I am having a dinner party on Friday 25 December at 7 pm. (India; A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2010; Panjabi; Pass)

Can you come to my house on Friday evening? (Iran; A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2010; Farsi; Pass)

My girlfriend likes vegetable pasta with tomato sauce, because she is vegetarian. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2006; Polish; Pass)

clusters.cluster_key: NOUNS | noun phrases - grammatical functions | FORM

rows.can_do: Can use nouns and noun phrases as subjects of the clause.
rows.example:
His name is Stanislav. (Singhalese, A1 BREAKTHROUGH)

My friends are from Poland, Hungary, Libya, and Morocco. (Slovak, A1 BREAKTHROUGH)

rows.can_do: Can use nouns and noun phrases as complements of prepositions in prepositional phrases.
rows.example:
I invite you and your family to come to my house for dinner. (Portuguese, A1 BREAKTHROUGH)

I like everything in my house. (Tamil, A1 BREAKTHROUGH)

rows.can_do: Can use nouns and noun phrases as objects of the clause.
rows.example:
I love my house. (Thai, A1 BREAKTHROUGH)

When I have time, I visit my family. (Portuguese, A1 BREAKTHROUGH)

rows.can_do: Can use nouns and noun phrases as adjuncts in some time expressions.
rows.example:
But if not, then I will see you tomorrow morning. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2009; Tamil; Pass)

I can go to see her tomorrow because I am going to a job interview today. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2009; Panjabi; Pass)

I will write to you again next week. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2007; Spanish - Other; Pass)

rows.can_do: Can use nouns and noun phrases as complements of the verb 'be'.
rows.example:
There is a big garden. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2009; Urdu; Pass)

Hi Rie, It's Mohamad. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2006; Kurdish; Pass)

He is a student in B6 College. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2006; Turkish; Pass)

rows.can_do: Can form plurals by adding '-s' to common countable nouns.
rows.example:
We play computer games. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2011; Polish; Pass)

You have to walk just 5 minutes down the road. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2008; Russian; Pass)

My favourite season is  summer, because it's very hot and there are a lot of flowers and nice trees. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2011; Hungarian; Pass)

rows.can_do: Can use proper nouns with a capital letter to refer to specific people, places, days and things.
rows.example:
I want to see you on Saturday and Sunday. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2009; Portuguese; Pass)

In Leicester I live in an area called Hamilton. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2009; Gujarati; Pass)

This place is Gloscat College in Gloucester. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2007; Tigrinya; Pass)

My friend is called Monika , she comes from Poland. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2006; Polish; Pass)

rows.can_do: Can use a limited range of common nouns.
rows.example:
My house is in Ilford. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2009; Tagalog; Pass)

In my free time I go shopping and I like swimming. (Poland; A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2010; Polish; Pass)

My friend is called Jessica. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2006; German; Pass)

rows.can_do: Can use a range of compound nouns, as one-word, two-word and hyphenated compounds, formed from verb + noun, noun + noun and adjective + noun combinations.
rows.example:
She likes to go to the swimming pool and to the gym. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2006; Ukrainian; Pass)

There are seven rooms: three bedrooms, a living room, a kitchen and there are two bathrooms. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2009; Farsi; Pass)

My house is next to the bus stop. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2009; Bengali; Pass)

My home is in Belfast, near the city centre. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2009; Italian; Pass)

There's a supermarket and  a post office . (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2007; Polish; Pass)

He likes sports like ice-hockey and table tennis. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2006; Singhalese; Pass)

I buy wines and ice-cream. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2006; Others; Pass)

rows.can_do: Can use the affirmative form with a limited range of regular and irregular verbs.
rows.example:
Every day at college I went to class at 9 am. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2007; Portuguese; Pass)

I met her about ten years ago. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2006; Others; Pass)

My grandmother lived in a village and I often went there. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2007; Polish; Pass)

rows.can_do: Can use the past simple to talk about everyday events or states.
rows.example:
The people were very polite. (A1, French)

Every time I went to Hendon Park, I saw so many people there. (A1, Polish)

I liked this place very much. (A1, French)

rows.can_do: Can form prepositional phrases with a preposition and a noun phrase.
rows.example:
When I have free time, I go to the park with my dog. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2007; Polish; Pass)

We have lots of vegetables and fruit in the summer. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2009; Slovak; Pass)

We sometimes meet friends or go to the Sports Center at the weekend. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2006; Mandarin; Pass)

rows.can_do: Can use a limited range of simple (single-word) prepositions.
rows.example:
I'm sending some flowers to you. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2009; Romanian; Pass)

He works in Bognor Regis. (A1 Slovak)

I can cook some food for you. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2009; Bengali; Pass)

I usually go shopping with her in Chester. (Bulgarian, A1 BREAKTHROUGH, Pass, 2006)

Can you come on Saturday at five o'clock in the afternoon? (A1 Polish)

I will come to your house on time. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2006; Mandarin; Pass)

rows.can_do: Can use the affirmative form.
rows.example:
I am sending you some flowers, because you are not well. (Bengali, A1 BREAKTHROUGH, 2009)

I am writing to tell you what I do every day at college. (Mandarin, ===NONE===, A1 BREAKTHROUGH, Pass, 2007)

rows.can_do: Can use the present continuous with a limited range of verbs to talk about situations and events in progress.
rows.example:
[in a message to someone who is ill] We are missing you. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2009; Nepali; Pass)

I am sending you these flowers because I think that you will like these pretty flowers and you will get well soon. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2009; Gujarati; Pass)

I really like it when we are doing more speaking. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2007; Kurdish; Pass)

rows.can_do: Can use the negative form with a limited range of regular and irregular verbs.
rows.example:
I don't like meat and fish. (Skills for life Elementary 1, A1, Kurdish)

You don’t know the place. (Skills for life Elementary 1, A1, French)

rows.can_do: Can use the affirmative form with a limited range of regular and irregular verbs.
rows.example:
Every day at college I learn new words and new things. (Skills for life Elementary 1, A1, Polish)

I am your new neighbour. (Skills for life Elementary 1, A1, Panjabi)

She plays tennis and she likes going to the swimming pool. (Skills for life Elementary 1, A1, French)

rows.can_do: Can use the present simple with the adverb 'really' as an intensifier. ► adverbs
rows.example:
I really like my kitchen, because it is big and new and it makes my cooking easy. (Skills for life Elementary 1; A1, Somali)

I really want to see you. (Skills for life Elementary 1, A1, Polish)

rows.can_do: Can use the present simple to talk about repeated events or habits, and general facts.
rows.example:
I work in a supermarket. (Skills for life Elementary 1, A1, Russian)

I like my place because it is quiet, the people are nice, it is near the town centre, not too far from the college and my flat is lovely. (Skills for life Elementary 1, A1, Kurdish)

It takes 25 minutes by car, but by bus it takes 1 hour … (Skills for life Elementary 1, A1, Nepali)

I watch TV in the evening. I read books at night. (Skills for life Elementary 1, A1, Farsi)

She goes to work by bus. (Skills for life Elementary 1, A1, Tamil)

rows.can_do: Can use a limited range of indefinite pronouns as objects.
rows.example:
I like doing everything at College because I love my college … (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2007; Arabic - Other; Pass)

I like to eat everything. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2006; Polish; Pass)

I can clean your house and cook something for you. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2009; Portuguese; Pass)

If you need anything, please let me know. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2009; Arabic - Other; Pass)

rows.can_do: Can use 'everything' as subject, with a singular verb.
rows.example:
I hope everything is all right. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2008; Polish; Pass)

[talking about the countryside] Everything is nice and green there. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2007; Slovak; Pass)

rows.can_do: Can use the pronouns 'you' and 'it' after 'be' in question forms.
rows.example:
Hi, Mrs. Bishop. How are you? (Skills for Life (Entry 1); ; A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2009; Tagalog; Pass)

Is it OK? (Skills for Life (Entry 1); ; A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2009; Romanian; Pass)

rows.can_do: Can use the pronoun 'it' as a direct object to refer to an object or situation already referred to.
rows.example:
The maths is boring and I don't like it. (Skills for Life (Entry 1); A1; Portuguese)

If you can't find it, call me. (Skills for Life (Entry 1); A1; Turkish)

Do you like it too? (Skills for Life (Entry 1); A1; German)

rows.can_do: Can use the object pronouns 'me', 'you', 'him', 'her', 'it', 'us' and 'them' in the object position after prepositions.
rows.example:
I really like to learn new words. It's very important for me. (Skills for Life (Entry 1); A1; Polish)

Sometimes I go with her. (Skills for Life (Entry 1); A1; Polish)

Can you bring some music so we can listen to it. (Skills for Life (Entry 1); A1; no L1 specified)

I spend my free time with them. (Skills for Life (Entry 1); A1; Lithuanian)

rows.can_do: Can use the pronouns 'I', 'you', 'we' and 'they' after an auxiliary or modal verb in question forms.
rows.example:
Can I help you? (Skills for Life (Entry 1); A1; Arabic – other)

What do you think about that? (Skills for Life (Entry 1); A1; French)

Can we have a chat? (Skills for Life (Entry 1); A1; Russian)

rows.can_do: Can use the pronouns 'me', 'you', 'him', 'her', 'us' and 'them' in the direct object position after a transitive verb.
rows.example:
I'm very happy that you want to see me. (Skills for Life (Entry 1); A1; German)

I can see her on Saturday at 8 pm. (Skills for Life (Entry 1); A1; Arabic)

It protects us from rain, wind and sun. (Skills for Life (Entry 1); A1; Portuguese)

You will find a lot of different shops … I hope you'll like them. (Skills for Life (Entry 1); A1; Russian)

rows.can_do: Can use the pronoun 'it' before 'be' to refer to a first person speaker or writer.
rows.example:
Hello Mrs Bishop. It's Clarisse (Skills for Life (Entry 1); A1; French)

Dear Cris, it's me, Paarth. (India; A2 WAYSTAGE; 2009; Hindi; Pass)

rows.can_do: Can use the pronouns 'I', 'you', 'he', 'she', 'it', 'we' and 'they' in the subject position before a verb in statements.
rows.example:
In my free time I go shopping and I like swimming. (Skills for Life (Entry 1); A1; Polish)

If you want, you can go inside. (Skills for Life (Entry 1); A1; Hindi)

He prefers chess. (Skills for Life (Entry 1); A1; Singhalese)

My friend's name is Julissa, she is from Ecuador and she lives near my home. (Skills for Life (Entry 1); A1; Spanish)

I like my home because it is a beautiful home. It has a big garden and a good sitting room. (Skills for Life (Entry 1); A1; Pashto)

I like my front garden because it's very nice and warm. (Skills for Life (Entry 1); A1; Czech)

They're nice people. (Skills for Life (Entry 1); A1; Arabic)

rows.can_do: Can use a limited range of modal verbs + subject + main verb to form 'yes/no' questions.  ► can
rows.example:
Can you come to dinner in my house? (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2008; Polish; Pass)

I can't find my CD, can you bring some music please? (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2008; Romanian; Pass)

rows.can_do: Can use linking verb 'be' with complements.
rows.example:
Because today is my birthday. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2008; Mandarin; Pass)

Martin is from Poland. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2006; Polish; Pass)

My house is very clean. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2009; Bengali; Pass)

rows.can_do: Can use a limited range of verbs followed by a 'to'- infinitive.
rows.example:
I like to go to the zoo. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2011; Arabic - Other; Pass)

Sometimes I don't like to go out because It's very cold. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2007; Tagalog; Pass)

I want to invite you for dinner. (India; A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2010; Panjabi; Pass)

rows.can_do: Can use 'would like to' + infinitive.
rows.example:
I would like to meet you to give you some information about the Brannl State and the shops near her. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2008; Arabic - Other; Pass)

I'd like to invite you on Sunday about six o'clock in the evening. (Poland; A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2010; Polish; Pass)

rows.can_do: Can use 'like' followed either by a 'to'-infinitive or an '-ing' form, with no change in meaning.
rows.example:
I like to work with my teacher. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2007; Italian; Pass)

I don't like to eat at College. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2007; Italian; Pass)

I like reading lots of magazines. (Hungary; A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2010; Hungarian; Pass)

I don't like doing homework in college. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2007; Thai; Pass)

I like learning grammar. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2007; Polish; Pass)

I like travelling by car. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2011; Others; Pass)

rows.can_do: Can use a limited range of prepositional verbs followed by noun or pronoun objects.
rows.example:
I listen to music in the living room, and also eat my breakfast, lunch and dinner. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2009; Dari; Pass)

I can look after her if she needs to do shopping and cleaning. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2009; Gujarati; Pass)

I like to walk in the woods and look for mushrooms. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2007; Polish; Pass)

rows.can_do: Can use 'there is' + singular noun phrase as complement.
rows.example:
My home is nice, there is a nice garden. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2007; Polish; Pass)

In my College there is a big library. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2007; Luganda; Pass)

In my home there are two bedrooms, there's a kitchen, there's a bathroom and a living-room. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2009; Spanish - Other; Pass)

rows.can_do: Can use 'there are' + plural noun phrase as complement.
rows.example:
There are two bathrooms. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2009; Farsi; Pass)

There are many shops and schools. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2007; Somali; Pass)

I think, in the park there are animals. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2011; Pashto; Pass)

rows.can_do: Can use auxiliary verb 'be'. ►  present continuous
rows.example:
I am writing to tell you what I do every day at college. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2007; Mandarin; Pass)

I'm working today. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2009; Turkish; Pass)

We are going to the park. (Somalia; A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2010; Somali; Pass)

rows.can_do: Can use a limited range of modal auxiliary verbs ('can', 'will', 'would').
rows.example:
Can you bring some music with you to the dinner. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2008; Bengali; Pass)

I'll be very happy to help you. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2009; Romanian; Pass)

I would like to eat some Spanish food. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2006; Italian; Pass)

rows.can_do: Can use linking verb 'be'.
rows.example:
I am 22 years old. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2008; Bengali; Pass)

There are 4 rooms. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2009; Tamil; Pass)

And I like my home because it's not too big and it's a very nice flat. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2009; Tamil; Pass)

It was very nice. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2007; Urdu; Pass)

rows.can_do: Can use regular and irregular main verbs in simple verb phrases. ► present simple ► past simple
rows.example:
You know, I love your cooking! (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2006; Slovak; Pass)

I have very good friends. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2009; Polish; Pass)

She comes from India. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2006; Nepali; Pass)

She has long, dark hair and brown eyes. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2006; Polish; Pass)

He gets up at 6 o'clock and goes to work. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2006; Polish; Pass)

I heard from our neighbours about you. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2009; Arabic - Other; Pass)

My grandmother lived in a village and I often went there. (A1 BREAKTHROUGH; 2007; Polish; Pass)
